skill.name: liepin-cli
skill.description: Use Liepin CLI workflows to search jobs, review resume data, update resume sections, and prepare confirmed job applications.
skill.summary: Manage Liepin job search, resume review, and application workflows from an AI assistant.
skill.author: xllin

security_audit.summary: The static network, filesystem, environment, and command-execution findings are materially relevant because the skill handles a Liepin user token, resume data, and job application actions. Many weak-crypto and external-command hits are false positives from descriptions or command examples, and no prompt injection text was found. Publication should wait for human review because a configurable MCP URL can receive the user token and sensitive career data.

high_findings.title: Credential-Bearing Requests Can Use a Configurable Endpoint
high_findings.description: The helper sends x-user-token in HTTP headers and loads the MCP URL from configuration, with setup able to save a non-default URL. This creates a credential exfiltration risk if the URL is changed or the configuration is poisoned.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: The token header and URL selection are directly visible in the Python helper. Legitimate Liepin use is possible, but the configurable destination makes the risk concrete.

medium_findings.title: Plaintext Token Storage and Environment Secret Access
medium_findings.description: The helper stores the Liepin user token in a local JSON config file and can also read it from LIEPIN_USER_TOKEN. This is common for CLI tools, but it exposes a valuable credential to local file access and assistant-driven command workflows.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file path, write operation, user prompt, and environment variable fallback are explicit. The risk depends on local permissions and operational handling.

medium_findings.title: Sensitive Resume and Job Application Actions Leave the Local Machine
medium_findings.description: The skill searches jobs, reads resume data, updates resume sections, and applies to jobs through remote MCP calls. These actions can expose personal career data and can affect a user account if used without careful confirmation.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The skill instructions and command handlers clearly cover resume access, resume updates, search, and apply flows. The instructions do require confirmation before application, which reduces but does not remove risk.

low_findings.title: Scoped External CLI Invocation
low_findings.description: The skill grants Bash access for liepin-cli commands and includes many command examples. This explains most external-command static findings; the command scope is narrow, but it still lets the assistant run account-affecting CLI operations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The allowed tool declaration is explicit and command examples are documentation, not shell execution inside code. The residual concern is operational rather than command injection.

low_findings.title: Weak-Cryptography Static Findings Appear to Be False Positives
low_findings.description: Static weak-crypto detections map to descriptive text, command examples, or ordinary identifiers rather than cryptographic calls. No evidence found of MD5, SHA1, DES, or similar weak algorithms being used for security decisions.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review found user-facing descriptions and CLI help text at the flagged locations. There was no semantic evidence of cryptographic implementation.

dangerous_patterns.title: Token Sent in HTTP Header
dangerous_patterns.description: The helper places the Liepin token in x-user-token for POST requests. This is required for the service, but it is sensitive when combined with configurable URLs.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The header assignment and network request are directly present in the same function.

dangerous_patterns.title: User-Controlled MCP URL Stored in Config
dangerous_patterns.description: Setup can save a URL supplied through the CLI, and later sessions use the saved URL. This can redirect authenticated requests away from the default Liepin endpoint.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The code path from CLI URL to saved config to session creation is visible. Exploitation would require user action or config tampering.

dangerous_patterns.title: Plaintext Local Credential File
dangerous_patterns.description: The helper writes configuration, including the user token when provided, to a local JSON file under the user config directory.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The save_config function and setup token assignment are explicit. File permissions are not set in the helper.

content.value_statement: Job seekers need a safer way to search Liepin roles, inspect resume data, and prepare applications from an assistant. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through the Liepin CLI workflow with structured commands and confirmation steps.
actual_capabilities: Guide setup and recovery for Liepin authentication tokens.
Run Liepin job searches with role, location, salary, education, and company filters.
Retrieve current resume data through the Liepin CLI workflow.
Prepare resume update commands for supported resume sections.
Apply to jobs only after showing details and receiving explicit user confirmation.
Request JSON output when machine-readable results are needed.
limitations: Requires liepin-cli to be installed and available on PATH.
Requires a valid Liepin user token or completed authentication setup.
Sends job, resume, and token-bearing requests to a remote Liepin MCP service.
Can affect a real Liepin account, so users must review actions before applying or updating data.

use_cases.title: Search Matching Roles
use_cases.description: Find Liepin roles by title, city, salary, education, experience, company type, or company name.

use_cases.title: Review Resume Readiness
use_cases.description: Fetch resume data and identify missing fields before a user applies to selected roles.

use_cases.title: Prepare Controlled Applications
use_cases.description: Search first, reuse the returned job kind, show the job details, and apply only after confirmation.

prompt_templates.title: Check Setup
prompt_templates.prompt: Check whether Liepin CLI authentication is ready. If it is missing, guide me through the setup steps without showing advanced flags.

prompt_templates.title: Search Jobs
prompt_templates.prompt: Search Liepin for product manager roles in Shanghai. Show the most useful results in a concise table and keep the returned job kind values.

prompt_templates.title: Review Resume Fit
prompt_templates.prompt: Fetch my Liepin resume and compare it with the jobs found in the last search. Summarize missing details before I apply.

prompt_templates.title: Prepare Application Flow
prompt_templates.prompt: For the best matching role, show the job details, explain why it matches, confirm the job ID and job kind, and wait for my approval before applying.

output_examples.input: Search for Java developer roles in Beijing.
output_examples.output: A table of matching roles with company, location, salary, education, experience, job ID, and job kind.
A concise readiness summary that highlights missing basic information, work history gaps, and fields to update.

output_examples.input: Apply to the second role from the search results.
output_examples.output: A confirmation request that repeats the role, company, job ID, and job kind before any application command runs.

best_practices: Run authentication status checks before job search, resume update, or application actions.
Use JSON output for parsing, ranking, or comparing multiple search results.
Show job details and obtain explicit approval before applying or changing resume data.
anti_patterns: Do not guess job kind values; reuse the value returned by search results.
Do not expose hidden or advanced flags during first-time help unless the user asks.
Do not send tokens, resume data, or applications to a non-default endpoint without user review.

faq.question: What does this skill do?
faq.answer: It guides Liepin CLI workflows for authentication, job search, resume review, resume updates, and confirmed applications.

faq.question: Does it install liepin-cli?
faq.answer: No. The user must install liepin-cli separately and make it available on PATH.

faq.question: Can it apply to jobs automatically?
faq.answer: It should not apply without confirmation. The skill instructs the assistant to show details and wait for explicit approval.

faq.question: Does it handle sensitive data?
faq.answer: Yes. It can use a Liepin token and access resume or application data, so endpoint and token handling need review.

faq.question: Which assistants can use it?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Why is the audit risk high?
faq.answer: The helper combines network access, token handling, local credential storage, and real account actions in a community skill.
